mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals a dovish policy pivot that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is already cooling, because rate cuts are then read as growth support rather than panic.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising rates, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable-to-lower.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks deteriorating labor-market momentum, which depresses earnings expectations and raises recession odds, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if Rank IC is non-negative across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which lifts near-term equity earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the signal turns negative during sustained demand expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with flat or declining CPIAUCSL signals improving real output without a fresh inflation shock, which should reduce rate-pressure fears and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are negative in most such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below its recent range identifies a housing-led demand slowdown that typically precedes broader consumption weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by > 10%
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is positive in most housing downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already restrictive and CPIAUCSL is decelerating, markets interpret high policy rates as likely to be cut soon, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days turn positive on average.
failure_condition: Reject if high FEDFUNDS periods with slowing CPIAUCSL do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than high FEDFUNDS periods with rising CPIAUCSL, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rise in UNRATE signals weakening payroll demand and rising slack, which should depress corporate revenue expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll weakness and rising unemployment do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS indicates broadening real activity and labor absorption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings breadth improves.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the effect vanishes outside recovery phases but appears elsewhere instead.
